reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): DISPUTED_FP triggered. The finding occurs in processDataColumnSidecarsFromExecution (subscriber_beacon_blocks.go:80-162), which processes data from the local Execution Layer via ReconstructDataColumnSidecars → GetBlobsV2 (engine_client.go:657-706). Line 706 explicitly states: 'We trust the execution layer we are connected to, so we can upgrade the read only data column sidecar into a verified one.' The Engine API is SEMI_TRUSTED per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json (authenticated via JWT). Phase 03's finding is factually correct: line 154 passes sidecar.Index directly instead of calling peerdas.ComputeSubnetForDataColumnSidecar(sidecar.Index) as done correctly in data_columns_reconstruct.go:198. On mainnet this is masked (128 subnets = 128 columns), but on networks with fewer subnets (e.g., 64 subnets, 128 columns), high column indices (64-127) would be used as subnet IDs, causing broadcast to unsubscribed/invalid subnets. However, exploiting this requires a compromised local EL to provide malicious blob data, which is outside the security model. Verdict: requires compromised SEMI_TRUSTED component (Engine API), outside security model. 01e reference: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-036, 'Publishing data columns must only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (custody/sampling)'.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Publishing data columns must only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (custody/sampling). Assertion: forall col: published(col, subnet) => subscribed(subnet, custody) || subscribed(subnet, sampling)
